---
title: "Quest 13: Iterative Loops - Pick the Right Loop"
subtitle: "Choose Between loop, while, and for"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🔄 QUEST 13 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/13-iterative-loops.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/13-iterative-loops.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Rust offers multiple loop styles for different goals.
Picking the right one becomes easier with practice.

## 💡 Practical Pattern

```rust
fn main() {
    let names = vec!["Ada", "Lin", "Noor"];
    for name in names {
        println!("Hi, {}", name);
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Use `while` to print countdown `3, 2, 1`.
After that, try printing `Lift off!` once the loop finishes.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut n = 3;
    while n > 0 {
        println!("{}", n);
        n -= 1;
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 13 Complete
You selected loop styles intentionally.
Next: [Quest 14: Non-Returning Functions](14-functions-non-returning.qmd)
:::
